title: Write Script Worksheet
text: Write Script Worksheet The Write Script Worksheet enables you to write ProcScript and JavaScript, and to edit objects shown in the Structure. In the Entity Editor, you can write generic ProcScript for controlling data I/O or common application behavior. In the Component Editor, you can code component-specific behavior for the component and its entities, occurrences, and fields. In Library editors, you can define IncludeScript and Global ProcScript modules and Snippets. The code that is ultimately compiled in a component may come from several sources: Locally-defined script written in the code containers of the component and its entities and fields Inherited script, defined in modeled entities and fields used by the component Generated script, coming from IncludeScript that is resolved during compilation Tools Resource Browser—depending on the editor, you add objects to the Structure using the Templates and Model tabs, or copy code from the Snippets tab. Script Editor—enables you to navigate to and edit up to two code containers simultaneously. Compiled Modules Inspector—provides a summary of the ProcScript modules for the selected object after compilation, and enables you to navigate to the module declaration, whether it is inherited, generated, or locally-defined. How to Find and Replace Strings in Code Split Views in the Script Editor Navigate to ProcScript modules Using the Uniface IDE Working with Development Objects Related Links Scripting Application Behavior Uniface enables you to script application behavior using ProcScript, Uniface's own scripting language, and JavaScript, the most widely-used scripting language for web applications. Snippets Snippets are named fragments of code or text that you can insert into your application script or layout to speed development. They are defined as development objects in snippet libraries. Compiled Module Information The Compiled Module Inspector in the Write Script worksheet provides insight into the code that is available in the runtime component. It displays a summary of the compiled modules and their sources. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
